user_query: Add a zucchini noodle pesto bowl dinner option for mother on day 4

fields.name: Zucchini Noodle Pesto Bowl
fields.short_description: Light, low-carb zucchini noodles tossed with a basil-parsley pesto, cherry tomatoes, toasted pine nuts, and a sprinkle of parmesan for a bright, vegetable-forward dinner.
fields.meal_suggestion: Use a spiralizer or vegetable peeler to make zucchini noodles (2 medium zucchinis per person). Make a quick pesto by blending fresh basil, parsley, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, toasted pine nuts (or walnuts), and a tablespoon of grated parmesan until smooth. Gently toss zucchini noodles with the pesto and halved cherry tomatoes; warm briefly in a skillet for 1–2 minutes if a slightly softened texture is preferred. Top with extra toasted pine nuts, a light dusting of parmesan, and a squeeze of lemon. For more protein, serve with grilled chicken, chickpeas, or a scoop of ricotta.
fields.nutrition_note: A low-carbohydrate, vegetable-forward meal rich in vitamins A and C from zucchini and tomatoes. Basil and parsley provide antioxidants; olive oil and pine nuts supply heart-healthy fats. Parmesan adds calcium and a small amount of protein. Pairing with a lean protein (grilled chicken, tofu, or chickpeas) increases satiety and balances macronutrients.

insertion_point.reasoning: Appended as an additional light, vegetable-forward dinner option complementary to the existing heavier and protein-focused dinner choices; position -1 places it after current dinner meals.
